name: Peppermint Mentha × piperita
summary: Peppermint (Mentha piperita), primarily as enteric-coated oil, is best documented for easing IBS-related abdominal pain and digestive discomfort through smooth-muscle relaxation and TRPM8 receptor activity, plus antimicrobial effects.
description: Peppermint (Mentha piperita), primarily as enteric-coated oil, is best documented for easing IBS-related abdominal pain and digestive discomfort through smooth-muscle relaxation and TRPM8 receptor activity, plus antimicrobial effects.
safetyNotes: Generally well tolerated for most users.

sources.title: A review of the bioactivity and potential health benefits of peppermint tea (Mentha piperita L.)
Ethnomedicinal, phytochemical and pharmacological updates on Peppermint (Mentha × piperita L.)-A review

dosage: Enteric-coated peppermint oil capsules (0.2-0.4 mL per capsule): 0.6-1.2 mL/day; evidence specific to enteric-coated formulations
typical_dosage: Enteric-coated peppermint oil capsules (0.2-0.4 mL per capsule): 0.6-1.2 mL/day; evidence specific to enteric-coated formulations
